item.title: "48 Hours" show schedule
item.description: True crime. Real justice. To miss it would be a crime.

item.title: Did a father use his 6-year-old son as an alibi for murder?
item.description: Reginald Reed Sr. said he was playing video games with his son, Reginald "Reggie" Reed Jr., when his wife, Selonia Reed, was killed. But detectives doubt his story.

item.title: The Day My Mother Never Came Home
item.description: Did a father use his 6-year-old son as an alibi for murder? A son grapples with his parents' troubled past. "48 Hours" contributor Vladimir Duthiers reports.

item.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
item.description: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

item.title: Detective believes 6-year-old's mother was killed in next room while he slept
item.description: For most of his life, Reggie Reed has wondered who murdered his mother Selonia Reed decades ago in Hammond, Louisiana. A fresh look at the evidence ultimately implicated the man he called his "rock" — Reginald Reed Sr., the man who lovingly raised him.

item.title: Utah declares emergency, limits fireworks as crews battle largest U.S. wildfire
item.description: Utah is restricting fireworks as the largest wildfire in the nation grows, fueled by dry conditions and gusting winds.

item.title: U.S., Iran trade more strikes as Trump threatens to "militarily complete the job"
item.description: The U.S. carried out a new round of strikes Sunday local time in response to "Iranian aggression against commercial shipping," CENTCOM says.

item.title: Letlow wins Louisiana GOP Senate runoff, AP projects
item.description: Louisianans voted Saturday in the state's Republican Senate runoff, as two candidates vied to replace Sen. Bill Cassidy, who did not receive enough votes in the primary to advance.

item.title: Venezuela death toll rises as search for earthquake survivors continues
item.description: Venezuelans have taken the search for missing loved ones into their own hands, citing a scarcity of government rescuers.

item.title: This week on "Sunday Morning" (June 28)
item.description: Jane Pauley hosts a special program celebrating our nation's semiquincentennial, "These United States - America at 250."

item.title: Sources say Taylor Swift has rented Madison Square Garden for possible wedding
item.description: Sources in City Hall say an application has been filed for an event in and around Madison Square Garden on Friday for 999 people, as speculation about Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding grows. Ali Bauman reports.

item.title: Rescue workers race against time in Venezuela after earthquakes
item.description: Rescuers are in a race against time in Venezuela to save survivors of the earthquakes that hit nearly three days ago. Rescue workers from Virginia and California have arrived to help. Camilo Montoya-Galvez reports.

item.title: 4 dead in Kentucky after heavy rain and floods, governor says
item.description: Four people have died due to flooding from thunderstorms in Kentucky, Gov. Andy Beshear said Saturday, and he issued a state of emergency with additional rainfall expected.

item.title: New volley of strikes between U.S. and Iran
item.description: U.S. forces have struck additional targets near the Strait of Hormuz, U.S. Central Command said. Earlier on Saturday, Iran struck another ship in the strait and took aim at a U.S. ally. Aaron Navarro reports from the White House.

item.title: High winds and heat fuel Utah's out-of-control wildfires
item.description: Several major wildfires burned across the western U.S. this weekend. Utah declared a state of emergency as scorching heat fueled the flames. Adam Yamaguchi reports and Andrew Kozak has the forecast.

item.title: Historian retraces Underground Railroad to mark America's 250-year journey
item.description: Anthony Cohen has spent his life bringing attention to the Underground Railroad, secret routes once used by enslaved people seeking freedom.

item.title: Retracing and remembering the Underground Railroad route
item.description: As the U.S. prepares to mark 250 years since its start, one man is making sure Americans remember and preserve the history of the Underground Railroad. Jericka Duncan has the story.

item.title: Hezbollah's leader rejects U.S.-Israeli brokered ceasefire
item.description: The leader of Hezbollah rejected the U.S.-Israel-brokered ceasefire. The deal would require Hezbollah to disarm, which Holly Williams reports would be difficult.

item.title: Video shows Boeing 777 making very low pass over Texas airfield
item.description: Data from FlightRadar24 showed the plane was no more than 25 feet above the ground during the low pass as it approached the Horseshoe Bay Resort Jet Center airport.

item.title: 6/27: CBS Weekend News
item.description: High winds and heat are fueling Utah's out-of-control wildfires; Iranian drones target Bahrain after U.S. strikes Iran.

item.title: Second Dan Sullivan eligible for ballot in Alaska Senate race, judge rules
item.description: A man with the same name and party affiliation as Alaska Republican U.S. Sen. Dan Sullivan is eligible to challenge the senator in the August primary, a judge ruled Friday.

item.title: Trump nominates former Oklahoma trooper Lance Schroyer to be ICE director
item.description: President Trump on Saturday said he has nominated Lance Schroyer to be the next director of U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement.

item.title: Michigan parents charged with murder after boy found to be 250+ pounds
item.description: Two Flint Township, Michigan, parents, are facing several charges, including second-degree murder, in the death of their 7-year-old son, who was 255 pounds and abused and neglected, according to the Genesee County prosecutor.

item.title: Germany, Denmark, Switzerland hit heat records as Europe sizzles
item.description: Germany hit a high of 106 degrees on Saturday, according to the country's national weather service.

item.title: Mom gets answers after fighting for diagnosis: "Something's wrong"
item.description: Michelle Williams struggled with high blood pressure and swelling for years before she was finally diagnosed with an unusual condition.

item.title: Buttigieg targeted by fake report to child protective services
item.description: Michigan State Police said law enforcement and Child Protective Services confirmed a report against Pete Buttigieg was unsubstantiated and false.

item.title: U.S. strikes targets in Iran after drone attack on cargo ship
item.description: The U.S. military says it hit Iranian targets over Iran's drone attack on a commercial vessel in the Strait of Hormuz, marking the first American strikes on Iran since the two countries formally agreed to extend a ceasefire last week.

item.title: Judge in Charlie Kirk murder case finds prosecutor in contempt
item.description: The defense team for Tyler Robinson asked that the death penalty be taken off the table following public comments by prosecutors.

item.title: WNBA suspends Alyssa Thomas for hitting Caitlin Clark's throat
item.description: No foul was initially called after Alyssa Thomas' fist made contact with Caitlin Clark's throat during a game between the Phoenix Mercury and Indiana Fever on Wednesday.

item.title: Heat waves push more Europeans to embrace air conditioning
item.description: Only 20% European homes have AC, compared to 90% in the U.S., but as the climate changes, that vast gulf may be set to shrink.
